code: test_queries <- function(con) { list(   tn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM touchstone")[1,1],   tddn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM touchstone_demographic_dataset")[1,1],   tcn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM touchstone_country")[1,1],   sdn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM scenario_description")[1,1],   sn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM scenario")[1,1],   been = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM burden_estimate_expectation")[1,1],   beoen = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM burden_estimate_outcome_expectation")[1,1],   becen = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM burden_estimate_country_expectation")[1,1],   rsn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM responsibility_set")[1,1],   rn = DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT count(*) FROM responsibility")[1,1] )} context("load")  testthat::test_that("Expected rows added", {   expect_equal(after$tn, before$tn + 1)   expect_equal(after$tddn, before$tddn + 23)   expect_equal(after$tcn, before$tcn + 946)   expect_equal(after$sdn, before$sdn)   expect_equal(after$sn, before$sn + 67)   expect_equal(after$been, before$been + 26)   expect_equal(after$beoen, before$beoen + 128)   expect_equal(after$becen, before$becen + 2137)   expect_equal(after$rsn, before$rsn + 17)   expect_equal(after$rn, before$rn + 127) })

what: Database connections (and where to find them).
title: Usage
text: use vaultr package, assume VAULT_ADDR VAULT_AUTH_GITHUB_TOKEN environment variables set - won’t go . read-connection production database used validate outcomes countries group’s expectations. get connection production:- get connection annex:-
code: vault <- vaultr::vault_client(login = "github")   password <- vault$read("/secret/vimc/database/production/users/readonly")$password   con <- DBI::dbConnect(RPostgres::Postgres(),                           dbname = "montagu",                           host = "production.montagu.dide.ic.ac.uk",                           port = 5432, password = password,                           user = "readonly") password <- vault$read("/secret/vimc/annex/users/vimc")$password   annex <- DBI::dbConnect(RPostgres::Postgres(),                           dbname = "montagu",                           host = "annex.montagu.dide.ic.ac.uk",                           port = 15432,                           password = password,                           user = "vimc")

previous_headings: Using stoner as a standalone package (without Dettl) > Uploading to the annex database
title: Usage
text: However, rather acquiring connections manually running ad hoc database queries annex, better express imports annex using dettl. imports made little complex usual length time taken data reduction, RAM require can large, possibility data replaced annex subsequent versions. Never--less, good formal process uploading data annex, dettl good way. example: Extract stage: read meta data, contain list groups locations different stochastic datasets processed. Also look necessary metadata files - responsibilities outcomes. Transform stage: Perform reduction, producing csv files. need dettl try validate output specific database tables, since often need create tables annex part upload. Load stage: Perform uploads created csv files, updating stochastic_file adding new tables annex.

title: Support for Building VIMC Montagu Touchstones, using Dettl
text: install.packages("stoner") Create montagu-import. Write CSV files folder called meta inside import folder, use stoner::stone_dump(con, touchstone, path) dump set CSVs existing touchstone, (carefully) modify. extract call stoner::stone_extract(path, con) test-extract call stoner::stone_test_extract(extracted_data) transform call stoner::stone_transform(extracted_data) test-transform call stoner::stone_test_transform(transform_data) load call stoner::stone_load(transformed_data, con) simplest case. also write load tests usual, can now concentrate entirely rows particular import expected add. See vignette detail.

title: Process stochastic data — stone_stochastic_process
text: Convert modelling group's stochastic files summary format, ready later uploading Montagu data annex. Four files produced reduce age -age-total, -5-total, calendar year, birth-cohort year.

title: Process stochastic data — stone_stochastic_process
code: stone_stochastic_process(   con,   modelling_group,   disease,   touchstone,   scenarios,   in_path,   files,   cert,   index_start,   index_end,   out_path,   pre_aggregation_path = NULL,   outcomes = list(deaths = "deaths", cases = "cases", dalys = "dalys", yll = "yll"),   dalys_recipe = NULL,   runid_from_file = FALSE,   allow_missing_disease = FALSE,   upload_to_annex = FALSE,   annex = NULL,   allow_new_database = FALSE,   bypass_cert_check = FALSE,   testing = FALSE,   lines = Inf,   log_file = NULL,   silent = FALSE )

title: Process stochastic data — stone_stochastic_process
text: con DBI connection production. Used verifying certificate expected properties modelling_group modelling group id disease disease touchstone touchstone (including version) estimates scenarios vector scenario_descriptions. files parameter length 1, must length number scenarios, one--one mapping two assumed. in_path folder containing stochastic files files Either single string containing placeholders indicate filenames, vector files, one scenario. Placeholders can include :group :touchstone :scenario :disease :index cert Name certificate file accompanying estimates index_start scalar vector matching length scenarios. entry either integer NA, indicating first number sequence files. NA implies single file sequence. placeholder :index filenames replaced . index_end Similar index_start, indicating last number sequence files. Can scalar, applying scenarios, vector entry scenario, integer value NA case. out_path Path writing output files pre_aggregation_path Path dir write pre age-disaggregated data . NULL skipped. outcomes list named vectors, name burden outcome, elements list column names stochastic files summed compute outcome. default expect outcomes deaths, cases, dalys, yll, single columns names stochastic files. dalys_recipe DALYs must calculated, can supply data frame , stoner calculate DALYs using recipe. data frame must names outcome, proportion, average_duration disability_weight. See stoner_calculate_dalys. runid_from_file Occasionally groups omitted run_id stochastic file, provided 200 files, one per run_id. Set runid_from_file TRUE case, deduce run_id filenames. index_start index_end must 1 200 case. allow_missing_disease Occasionally groups omitted disease column stochastic data. Set TRUE expect circumstance, avoid generating warnings. upload_to_annex Set TRUE want upload results straight annex. (Files still created, upload relatively fast; creating csvs slower worth caching) annex DBI connection annex, used upload_to_annex TRUE. allow_new_database uploading, set TRUE enable creating stochastic_file table found. bypass_cert_check TRUE, checks carried parameter certificate (provided). testing internal use . lines Number lines read file, Inf default read lines. Set lower number testing subset process full run. log_file Path file save logs , NULL log file. file exists appended , otherwise file created. silent TRUE silence console logs.

title: Standardise stochastic data files — stone_stochastic_standardise
text: Convert modelling group's stochastic files intermediate format including original data, separated country scenario, PQ format later processing. much possible, try detect input formats.

title: Standardise stochastic data files — stone_stochastic_standardise
code: stone_stochastic_standardise(   group,   in_path,   out_path,   scenarios,   files,   index = 1,   rubella_fix = TRUE,   missing_run_id_fix = TRUE )

title: Standardise stochastic data files — stone_stochastic_standardise
text: group modelling group. in_path folder network path original files found. out_path folder network path write output files . scenarios vector strings giving scenario name. files can either vector strings length vector scenarios, case entry match scenario, providing filename original uploads scenario. groups provide files numbered 1 200 stochastic runs; replace number :index filename match. Alternatively, files can single entry containing string :scenario; case, files must exist match entry scenarios parameter, file string can used match (perhaps additionaly wiht :index). index usually vector ints, 1:200 match range stochastic files uploaded per scenario. groups upload one large file containing everything, case :index occur files parameter, omitted. rubella_fix Historically Rubella uploads used burden outcome rubella_deaths_congenital rubella_cases_congenital instead simpler deaths cases, additionally provided rubella_infections field. rubella_fix needs TRUE standardise simpler names. Processing Rubella stochastic files without set TRUE fail - always , keeping parameter makes clear code . missing_run_id_fix groups past omitted run_id files, included filenames. fix inserts files index parameter indicates 200 runs process.

what: Calculating DALYs for stochastic or central estimates — stoner_calculate_dalys
title: Calculating DALYs for stochastic or central estimates — stoner_calculate_dalys
text: One modelling group provide DALYs, need calculate , central estimates (already uploaded Montagu), stochastic estimates (provided CSV files). DALYs calculation simple function life expectancy people (given age country given year), various weighted contributions different burden outcomes.

title: Calculating DALYs for stochastic or central estimates — stoner_calculate_dalys
code: stoner_calculate_dalys(   con,   touchstone,   data,   dalys_params,   life_table = NULL,   year_min = 2000,   year_max = 2100 )

title: Calculating DALYs for stochastic or central estimates — stoner_calculate_dalys
text: con DBI connection Montagu database. Used retrieving demographic data life expectancy. touchstone touchstone (including version); demographic data retrieved specific touchstone. data data.frame containing burden estimates - either central stochastic. columns kinds include country (may Montagu numerical id, 3-letter code), year, age, number burden outcomes.Stochastic data also run_id. dalys_params data.frame containing calculate dalys; row represents condition, four columns describe calculate cost condition across population year. condition, outcome column must provide burden outcome (column name data). proportion proportion people reported outcome, suffer condition. average_duration number years condition suffered, set greater 120 life-long period. disability_weight measure severe implications particular condition. See http://ghdx.healthdata.org/record/ihme-data/gbd-2017-disability-weights figures come ; final figures decided discussion groups. life_table NULL, life table looked interpolated yearly age time. takes bit time, resulting data part function returns, life table part can provided speed things . provided, data.frame columns value .code; latter format country-year-age. type country .code match type country data. years ages interpolated single-years. year_min first year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2000) year_max final year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2100)

title: Calculating DALYs for stochastic or central estimates — stoner_calculate_dalys
text: list two named components; life_table result calling life-table function, can specified subsequent calls stoner_calculate_dalys, data version original dataset, now dalys column. Note data already contained dalys column provided function, column overwritten new results.

title: Create CSV with DALYs for an uploaded burden estimate set — stoner_dalys_for_db
text: con DBI connection Montagu database, retrieving burden_estimate set. dalys_params data.frame containing calculate dalys; row represents condition, four columns describe calculate cost condition across population year. condition, outcome column must provide burden outcome (column name data). proportion proportion people reported outcome, suffer condition. average_duration number years condition suffered, set greater 120 life-long period. disability_weight measure severe implications particular condition. See http://ghdx.healthdata.org/record/ihme-data/gbd-2017-disability-weights figures come ; final figures decided discussion groups. modelling_group modelling group, disease, touchstone scenario provided, lookup current (recently uploaded) burden estimate set matching details. disease specified, name disease question touchstone specified, touchstone (including version) set. scenario specified, name scenario set. burden_estimate_set_id Alternatively, modelling_group, disease, touchstone scenario NULL, numerical id particular burden estimate set can specified , extend estimate set DALYs. Otherwise, leave NULL look four fields. output_file provided, write output pq file additional DALYs field filename. file ready uploaded Montagu. life_table provided, re-use life table provided previous call function. Otherwise, can left NULL generate life-table. year_min first year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2000) year_max final year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2100)

title: Create CSV with DALYs for an uploaded burden estimate set — stoner_dalys_for_db
text: list two components, data data.frame complete DALYs extended data, life_table life table used calculating DALYs, required .

text: Normally called internally stoner_calculate_dalys, testing purposes also exposed.

title: Calculate life expectancy table — stoner_life_table
code: stoner_life_table(con, touchstone, year_min, year_max, char_countries = TRUE)

title: Calculate life expectancy table — stoner_life_table
text: con DBI connection Montagu database. Used retrieving demographic data life expectancy. touchstone touchstone (including version); demographic data retrieved specific touchstone. year_min first year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2000) year_max final year range calculate DALYs. (Default 2100) char_countries logical TRUE specifies countries resulting life table expressed 3-character form, otherwise numerical.

title: Calculate life expectancy table — stoner_life_table
text: data.frame columns value .code. value expected years life left person particular country, particular year, particular age. .code form country-year-age, year age interpolated single years (whereas UNWPP life table data mostly 5-year bands).
